rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainant Belrose, Llc And John D. Kaufmans Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Defendant Andrew Chius Demurrer To The Fifth Amended Cross-Complaint And Request For Sanctions; And Supporting Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2021 line 1. Cross-Complainant Belrose, LLC And John D. Kaufman's Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Defendant Andrew Chiu's Demurrer To The Fifth Amended Cross-Complaint And Request For Sanctions is DENIED. The Court strongly admonished all parties that meet and confer efforts are supposed to be undertaken in good faith. Late attempts and refusals to proceed with meeting and conferring are not in the spirit of the CCP and do not advance the matters. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY's DEMURRER to 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for negligence is sustained without leave to amend. Demurrer to the causes of action for breach of contract and breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. For the purposes of the demurrer, cross-complainant's feasible interpretation of the contract must be accepted. Paragraph 4.4. of the Escrow instructions suggests that "Capital Funding" and "undersigned" are not one and the same as is urged by the moving party. "Your written request for funds specifying the anticipated business day of recording shall be communicated to the undersigned and Capital Funding." =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT PATRICK SPIERS' DEMURRER to 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT The court is inclined to sustain the demurrer. By no later than 8:30 a.m. on March 10, 2021 cross-complaint to submit a supplemental brief limited to the facts that may be alleged if leave to amend is granted. Such facts must be provided in a table format for each cause of action: (1) column one - element of the cause of action; (2) column two- non conclusory facts re: Defendant Spiers in support of the element. No further argument is allowed. rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT DAMIEN QUINN's MOTION TO STRIKE 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party appears to base its motion to strike on demurrer-like arguments (failure to attach a contract; failure to allege fraud with specificity). These are not proper grounds for the motion to strike. Furthermore, in the event allegations in the complaint are contradicted by the exhibits attached thereto, the exhibits control. There is no basis for striking the allegations. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 10, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT DAMIEN QUINN' DEMURRER to 5TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED in part. Hearing Required in part. By no later than 8:30 a.m. on March 10, 2021 cross-complaint to submit a supplemental brief limited to the facts that may be alleged if leave to amend is granted and citation to authorities as specified below. Such facts must be provided in a table format for RICO violation cause of action: (1) column one - element of the cause of action; (2) column two- non conclusory facts re: Defendant Quinn in support of the element. Cross-Complainant shall further provide citations to authorities standing for the proposition that oral claims of ownership/refusal of entry constitute "publication" within the meaning of slander of title cause of action. No further argument is allowed. Inclusion on unauthorized briefing will result in the brief being stricken in its entirety. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
